title: What is hoisting in JavaScript?
content: Explain hoisting in JavaScript. How does it work with var, let, const, and function declarations?

title: Difference between == and ===
content: What's the difference between == and === operators in JavaScript? Provide examples.

title: What are closures?
content: Explain closures in JavaScript. Provide examples and discuss their practical applications.

title: What is the event loop?
content: Explain the JavaScript event loop. How does asynchronous execution work in JavaScript?

title: What are arrow functions?
content: Explain arrow functions in ES6. How do they differ from regular functions, especially regarding 'this' binding?

title: What is JavaScript?
content: JavaScript is a lightweight, interpreted programming language used to make web pages interactive.

title: Data types in JavaScript
content: String, Number, Boolean, Undefined, Null, Symbol, BigInt, and Object.

title: Difference between var, let, and const
content: var: function scoped, let: block scoped, const: block scoped with immutable reference.

title: What are JavaScript objects?
content: Objects are collections of key-value pairs used to store data and methods.

title: Program: reverse a string
content: Take a string input and print the reversed version of it.

title: What is an array in JavaScript?
content: An array is a special object used to store multiple values in a single variable.

title: Program: largest number in an array
content: Given an array of numbers, find and display the maximum value.

title: What are JavaScript functions?
content: Functions are blocks of reusable code designed to perform a specific task.

title: Program: Fibonacci series
content: Display the first n terms of the Fibonacci sequence.

title: Difference between null and undefined
content: undefined means a variable has been declared but not assigned; null is an assignment value meaning empty.

title: Program: palindrome check
content: Take a string input and check if it reads the same forward and backward.

title: What are JavaScript events?
content: Events are actions in the browser (like clicks, key presses) that can be handled with code.

title: Program: count vowels in a string
content: Read a string and count the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u).

title: What is the DOM in JavaScript?
content: The Document Object Model represents the structure of HTML/XML documents that can be accessed and manipulated with JavaScript.

title: Program: sort an array of numbers
content: Take an array and sort its elements in ascending order.

title: What are JavaScript promises?
content: Promises represent the result of an asynchronous operation, with states: pending, fulfilled, or rejected.

title: Program: factorial of a number
content: Take an integer n and calculate its factorial using a loop.

title: Program: find duplicate elements in array
content: Write a JavaScript program to find duplicate elements in an array.

title: Object iteration
content: Write JavaScript code to iterate through all properties of an object and print keys and values.

title: Form validation
content: Write JavaScript code to validate a form ensuring name is not empty and email is valid.

title: Program: sum of array elements
content: Write a JavaScript program to calculate the sum of all elements in an array.

title: Program: prime number check
content: Write a JavaScript function to check whether a number is prime.
